title: Grammar · English B1
scope: B1 (Cambridge Preliminary) consolidates the present perfect vs past simple, the past continuous, first and second conditionals, used to, the passive in simple tenses, defining relative clauses, an introduction to reported speech, and verb patterns (gerunds and infinitives). Boundary: you handle real and hypothetical situations, but advanced tenses and inversion come later.

blocks.text: Past simple = finished time (yesterday, in 2010). Present perfect = unfinished time or a link to now (for, since, ever, never).

rows: I have seen that film twice.
I have lived here for three years / since 2021.

blocks.text: was/were + verb-ing, for an action in progress in the past, often interrupted by a past simple action.
I was cooking when the phone rang. While we were talking, it started to rain.
Real or likely future situations: If + present simple, will + base verb.
If it rains, I will stay at home. If you study, you will pass.
Unreal or unlikely present/future situations: If + past simple, would + base verb.
If I had more time, I would travel more. If I were you, I would accept.
were is used for all persons in the conditional: If I were rich…
used to + base verb for past habits or states that are no longer true.
I used to play football. She didn't use to like coffee. Did you use to live here?

sections.title: Modals: might, could, should, ought to

rows: We could go to the cinema.
You should rest. You ought to apologise.

blocks.text: be + past participle, when the action matters more than the doer. The agent, if mentioned, uses by.
This book was written by a famous author.
Give essential information about a noun, using who (people), which (things), that (both), where (places).
The man who lives next door is a doctor. This is the book that I told you about.
When reporting what someone said, the tense usually shifts back one step.

rows: She said she would call me.

blocks.text: Some verbs are followed by -ing, others by to + base verb.
